title: ESCALATE: Context hook uses non-atomic writeFileSync

text: {"type":"escalate","finding":"Context hook uses non-atomic writeFileSync","severity":"Important","description":"tmux-status-context-hook.js:55 writes the bridge file using writeFileSync() directly to the final path, rather than temp+rename. Violates the project's 'atomic writes everywhere' convention (CLAUDE.md and DESIGN.md). Risk is low (small payload, fast writes) but inconsistent.","options":[{"label":"Option 1: Add temp+rename pattern","solution":"Write to bridgePath + '.tmp' then fs.renameSync(tmpPath, bridgePath). 2 extra lines.","pros":"Matches atomic write convention documented in CLAUDE.md and DESIGN.md, consistent with all other bridge file writers","cons":"2 extra lines of code"},{"label":"Option 2: Accept as known exception","solution":"Document the inconsistency. Payload is <100 bytes, partial reads extremely unlikely.","pros":"No code change","cons":"Inconsistent convention"}],"recommendation":"Option 1 — trivial fix, consistency matters","promotion_reason":"Natural FIX promoted to ESCALATE — max fix cycles (3) reached after 4 cycles"}
